headline: Oklahoma's defense strangles Colorado in low-scoring battle
body: Oklahoma's 22-10 victory over Colorado on Saturday was a masterclass in defensive suffocation, even if the scoreboard never quite reflected the completeness of the performance. The Sooners held Colorado to just 10 points despite the Buffaloes outgaining them in passing yards 306 to 256, a sign that the visiting defense made crucial stops when yardage threatened to become points. Colorado's two fumbles—both of which Oklahoma recovered—ultimately proved the difference in a game that turned into a field position battle.

QB Glass managed the game without spectacular numbers, completing 28 of 42 passes for 266 yards as Oklahoma's offense leaned on a steadier diet of runs. That conservative approach yielded just 127 rushing yards, yet it controlled clock and tempo in a way that limited Colorado's possessions. The Buffaloes countered with Anderson's 300-yard effort, including a touchdown throw, and Golden's reliable seven receptions for 94 yards suggested they had weapons Oklahoma's secondary couldn't completely shut down. Yet those yard totals proved deceiving—Colorado never found the end zone again after one score.

The game's real narrative belonged to the kicking game and turnover margin. Keith Rocha's four field goals accounted for nearly all of Oklahoma's production, and while that doesn't sound like championship football, it underscores how well the Sooners' defense corralled Anderson and the Buffaloes' receivers in the red zone. Colorado's inability to protect the football—two lost fumbles against zero—essentially handed Oklahoma the game in the fourth quarter when field position mattered most.
turningPoint: Colorado's second fumble midway through the fourth quarter, recovered by Oklahoma's defense deep in Buffaloes territory, ended any momentum the visitors had built and forced them into a two-possession deficit with time running short.

unsungHero.name: Phillips
unsungHero.why: His six tackles anchored Colorado's defense and kept the Buffaloes competitive despite the offense struggling to convert yardage into points, yet the turnovers and field goal drought overshadowed his steady work on the backend.
